title: Thermal Cycle (BCTDS Before and After)
description: Raw BCTDS homodyne I/Q measurements of single-pulse broadband spectroscopy on a sapphire sample coated with Shipley 1813 photoresist, taken across two separate cooldowns three days apart without breaking the dilution fridge vacuum or changing the measurement setup. The drive frequency is swept from 2000 to 4999 MHz in 1 MHz steps. Comparing the two cooldowns shows how thermal cycling to room temperature repopulates the TLS defects, changing both the transient amplitude spectra and the phase V features. Only the in-phase (I) and quadrature (Q) signals are measured; magnitude, phase, and FFT spectra are computable from them and are not stored.

samples.name: Shipley
samples.material: Sapphire substrate with spin-coated Shipley 1813 photoresist

cooldowns.description: after thermal cycling to room temperature (cooldown 2)

apparatus.temperature_note: Approximate (~22 mK), and identical for both cooldowns. Not recorded in the raw files.
apparatus.waveguide: WR-229 rectangular waveguide, 3-6 GHz passband, TE10 mode

pulse_geometry.frame: raw-trace samples/ns, the same frame as timestamp_ns
pulse_geometry.source: Measured from the data (threshold at 30% of peak magnitude), NOT declared in generate_fig_13.py. Threshold-based edges overestimate the extent slightly. This is a single-pulse experiment: there is no inter-pulse spacing or phase sweep.

constant_drive_parameters.pulse_width_note: NOT RECORDED. Neither the filename nor generate_fig_13.py states the pulse duration, so this dataset has no pulse_width_ns column. The measured envelope (~65 -> ~105 ns) is wider than the 31.33 ns (308-tick) pulse used in experiments 3, 4, 5, 11 and 12, so that value is not assumed here.
